name: ent (Go ORM)
what_it_does: Type-safe Go ORM framework using code generation. Define data schema in Go structs, run go generate, and get a fully type-safe querying API with no reflection. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, and TiDB. Developed by Facebook (Meta) and used in production at scale. Graph-based querying for complex relationship traversal.
use_cases: Build type-safe Go data access layers with schema defined in Go code and generated query builders
Implement complex entity relationship queries using ent's graph traversal API (edges, paths)
Auto-generate database migrations from ent schema changes using atlas integration
Build multi-tenant applications with ent's hook system for automatic tenant isolation
Implement audit logging and soft deletes with ent's interceptor and hook system
not_for: Teams preferring raw SQL — use sqlc for SQL-first code generation; ent is schema-first
Flexible dynamic queries — ent's type-safe API is less flexible than raw SQL for highly dynamic conditions
best_when: Go services with complex entity relationships where type-safe graph traversal and schema-first development matter.
avoid_when: You prefer SQL-first development or need high flexibility in query construction — use sqlc or GORM.

auth.notes: ORM library — no auth needed. Database auth via connection string.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed Facebook/Meta open source project.

performance.rate_limits: None — local library. Generated code has minimal overhead vs raw SQL.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Type-safe querying surfaces most errors at compile time. Runtime errors include not-found errors (IsNotFound), constraint violations with DB-level details.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CRUD operations follow standard database semantics. Create is not idempotent; Upsert (CreateOrUpdate) is.
known_agent_gotchas: Schema changes require running go generate — ent generates code from schema; changed schema fields won't be accessible until regeneration
ent migrations are separate from schema — use atlas (entgo.io/ent/dialect/sql/schema) for migration generation; don't assume schema.Create auto-migrates safely in production
Edge (relationship) loading is explicit — ent does NOT lazy-load edges; must use .WithX() eager loading or explicit .QueryX() calls
ent uses int by default for primary keys — switch to UUID or other types via schema.DefaultFunc or field.UUID() for distributed systems
Hooks and interceptors run for all mutations — test hooks carefully; accidentally recursive hooks cause infinite loops
ent v0.12+ changed the entc.Generate API — older entc.go examples may not compile with current versions

security_score_components.security_notes: Generated parameterized queries prevent SQL injection. Schema-level field validation reduces invalid data entry. Hooks enable row-level security patterns.
